title: Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern
body:
## Fix tvOS native build: split device/simulator and fix GN args

### Problem

The tvOS native build had three interconnected issues preventing tvOS simulator apps from working:

1. **Combined device+simulator output** — All tvOS builds output to a single `output/native/tvos/` directory, mixing device (arm64) and simulator (x86_64) archives. The NuGet package only contained `runtimes/tvos/` with no `runtimes/tvossimulator/` entry, so .NET could not resolve native libraries for the simulator.

2. **Missing arm64 simulator build** — Only x86_64 simulator was built. Apple Silicon Macs running the tvOS simulator need arm64 simulator binaries.

3. **Wrong GN arg for min deployment target** — The GnNinja call used `min_ios_version` instead of `min_tvos_version`. Since Skia only reads `min_tvos_version` when `target_os='tvos'`, the arg was silently ignored and Skia fell back to its default of 9.0 instead of the intended 11.0.

### Fix

Mirrors the existing iOS pattern (`native/ios/build.cake`) which already correctly separates device and simulator:

**`native/tvos/build.cake`:**
- Split `OUTPUT_PATH` from `output/native/tvos` → `output/native` (with `tvos/` and `tvossimulator/` subdirs)
- Added `appletvsimulator arm64` build for Apple Silicon simulator support
- Added `ios_use_simulator` GN flag for simulator builds
- Added `SKIA_PLATFORM` override to `RunXCodeBuild` for libSkiaSharp (selects correct Skia static lib path)
- Changed `min_ios_version` → `min_tvos_version` (Skia has separate GN args per platform)
- SafeCopy simulator x86_64 archive to `tvos/` dir (backward-compat fat framework, same as iOS)

**`native/tvos/libSkiaSharp/libSkiaSharp.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`:**
- Added `SKIA_PLATFORM = tvos` as default build setting (overridden by build.cake at build time)
- Changed `LIBRARY_SEARCH_PATHS` from hardcoded `skia/out/tvos/$(ARCHS)` to `skia/out/$(SKIA_PLATFORM)/$(ARCHS)`

**`binding/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS/` and `binding/HarfBuzzSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS/`:**
- Added `runtimes/tvossimulator/native/` PackageFile entries to include simulator frameworks in NuGet

### Design notes

**Why `SKIA_PLATFORM = tvos` in the xcodeproj?**
This is a *default* value, not a hardcode. `build.cake` overrides it at build time via `RunXCodeBuild` properties — passing `tvos` for device builds and `tvossimulator` for simulator builds. The default just ensures the project opens correctly in Xcode without the build script. This is identical to how iOS does it (`SKIA_PLATFORM = ios` default, overridden to `iossimulator`).

**Why doesn't HarfBuzz need `SKIA_PLATFORM`?**
Only `libSkiaSharp.xcodeproj` has `LIBRARY_SEARCH_PATHS` pointing to `skia/out/$(SKIA_PLATFORM)/$(ARCHS)` because it links against Skia's static libraries. HarfBuzz is self-contained — it doesn't reference any Skia output paths, so it doesn't need the variable.

**Why copy simulator x86_64 into the device (`tvos/`) directory?**
This creates a legacy-style fat framework with device arm64 + simulator x86_64 for backward compatibility. iOS does the exact same thing. The proper simulator-only framework lives in `tvossimulator/`. .NET runtime selection picks the correct directory (`tvos/` for device, `tvossimulator/` for simulator).

**Why `min_tvos_version` instead of `min_ios_version`?**
Skia's GN build system has separate deployment target args per platform: `min_ios_version`, `min_tvos_version`, `min_maccatalyst_version`. When `target_os='tvos'`, only `min_tvos_version` is read (for `-mtvos-version-min` and `-target` flags). The old code passed `min_ios_version` which was silently ignored, causing tvOS to build with Skia's default of 9.0. The Xcode framework build was already using 11.0 (via `TVOS_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET`), so this fix makes GnNinja consistent with Xcode.

### Verification

CI build verified:
- ✅ Native artifacts: `tvos/` (arm64+x86_64 fat) and `tvossimulator/` (arm64+x86_64 fat) frameworks present
- ✅ NuGet packages: both `runtimes/tvos/` and `runtimes/tvossimulator/` included
- ✅ All other platforms unaffected (Windows, Linux, WASM, macOS, iOS all green)
